title: A topological loop is generated after using async2sync
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.36+72 

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps


Here is the source file: [reproduce.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/15433745/reproduce.tar.gz).

After downloading the source file from the attachment, you can use the following commands to reproduce the issues:
`tar -xzvf reproduce.tar.gz`
`cd reproduce/`
`yosys or_1200.ys`



### Expected Behavior

A Btor file should be generated successfully.

### Actual Behavior

After using `async2sync`, a logic loop was detected by `scc`. The following figure shows the result of detecting the logic loop **before** using the command `async2sync`.
![image](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/assets/103818428/4995892f-6ff1-4862-a2a3-0f1f850502f6)
And this figure indicates the result of detecting the logic loop **after** using the command `async2sync`.
![image](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/assets/103818428/5cd454b7-1e27-4ed1-99fa-302cce3cddab)
We can see that, after applying `async2sync`, a logic loop was synthesized, which led to an error (Found topological loop) when I  tried to generate a BTOR file:
![image](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/assets/103818428/c11d3d85-1184-4c2c-b575-2e6ce06698e6)
Another interesting phenomenon is that, in the original RTL file, we changed the width of the state variable `coverage_` and modified the index of the  `coverage_` to be assigned in the _always_ block, then a BTOR file was generated successfully. You can run the following command to get this result:
                                            `yosys or_1200_assert.ys`
Compared to the original file, the changes are only the width of the state variable `coverage_` and the index of the  `coverage_` to be assigned, but no logic loop is produced in this case, thus we can obtain a BTOR file finally.
